_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Implement AI application security controls, including prompt injection defenses, output filtering, access controls, and audit logging.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-T-009 (DA, cosine: 0.427), TF-A-002 (TF, cosine: 0.412).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.9/10 (raw 0.291). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [1, 2, 3, analysis, malware, perform] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, ai, application, audit, controls, defenses] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.862 (P=0.867, R=0.858). Balanced P/R — the 8-word DCWF element and 16-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.8%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Perform tier 1, 2, and 3 malware analysis." and "Implement AI application security controls, including prompt injection defenses,". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.434 > STS 0.291 (gap +0.143). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 4.5%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [1, 2, 3, analysis, malware, perform]. WIDAI terms: [access, ai, application, audit, controls, defenses].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.8% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.143 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
